{"step_by_step_thinking": "The query is about the reason for a labial surface perforation just above the cementoenamel junction (CEJ) during access opening of a maxillary incisor. The relevant information comes from the MEDCORP document titled 'Endodontics, Pulp Space Anatomy and Access Cavity of Anterior Teeth -- Technique -- Traditional technique complications'. The document mentions that misjudgment of the tooth's lingual-axial or mesial-axial inclination can result in abrasion of the labial or distal wall during instrumentation. This suggests that the inability to assess the lingual inclination of the crown to the root can lead to such perforations.
